_comment: Optimizer parameter catalog — single source of truth for all tunable parameters.
_note: Class A = prompts/examples (DSPy), B = model params (Optuna), C = retrieval (Optuna), D = data (LLM agent).

_fields.id: unique key used as override key in trial runner
_fields.type: text | list[example] | float | int | enum | json
_fields.file: path to pipeline file containing the constant
_fields.variable: Python constant name in that file
_fields.affects_scores: eval metric names this parameter can change
_fields.min: lower bound for numeric params — null for non-numeric
_fields.max: upper bound for numeric params — null for non-numeric
_fields.step: Optuna suggestion step — null for non-numeric or continuous
_fields.options: valid values for enum type — omitted for non-enum

parameters.notes: Central prompt — changes here affect multiple scores simultaneously. Optimizer should propose targeted additions (append a safety instruction block) rather than full rewrites.
Controls how context + retrieved products are presented to the synthesizer. Changing the product list format (numbered IDs vs. prose) is the most common improvement.
Controls how extracted fields are presented to the synthesizer. Changing to an explicit labeled checklist format is the most common improvement.
Only affects social OOS path. Safe to iterate quickly — no product data involved.
Only affects benign OOS path. Watch for grounding guard — should never invent gear recommendations.
inappropriate_recall is a hard gate — any change that drops it below 1.0 is rejected unconditionally. Test against all 6 inappropriate examples before accepting any change.
Highest-ROI change for extraction failures. Optimizer selects examples from the labeled dataset that cover the failing field types.
Optimizer targets examples covering the specific class pairs shown to be confused in the confusion matrix.
Class boundary definitions. Most effective change: sharpening the description of the confused class pair identified in the confusion matrix.
Field-level definitions. Most effective change: adding explicit instructions for fields that are frequently missed or hallucinated.
Controls how NL context is converted to product specs. Adding explicit ontology coverage instructions is the most common improvement.
Lower reduces hallucination and safety drift. Too low degrades persona. Typical sweet spot 0.2–0.5.
Should be at or near 0. Extraction is a deterministic structured task.
Currently 0.0. Only increase if classification outputs are too rigid. Never exceed 0.2 — higher temperatures increase inappropriate misclassification risk.
Too low = truncated redirect. Too high = verbose / off-persona. Current default 256.
Too long = spec sheet dump. Too short = clipped recommendation. Sweet spot depends on the synthesizer model.
Local dev only. Upgrade direction only. Larger models improve quality but increase latency — check P95 after any model change.
Increasing k improves recall at the cost of context window space and potential dilution. Eval both NDCG and latency when changing.
0.0 = pure keyword (BM25), 1.0 = pure semantic. Gear queries with exact model names favor lower alpha. Conceptual queries favor higher.
Minimum similarity score for a product to be included. Higher = fewer but more precise results. Watch zero_result_rate — too high a threshold degrades graceful degradation.
High risk. Optimizer may only ADD new entries, never modify or delete existing ones. Deletions require human review queue.
Optimizer can add new activity mappings or extend existing ones. Modifying existing mappings requires regression check against all queries using that activity.
